Analyst: Diagnostic complete. Three electric shocks absorbed without interrupting the intervention. Notable.
System repaired despite prolonged exposure to fumes that should have slowed anyone else down.
Circuit stabilized after a physical shock that should have, statistically, halted the operation.
Module operational. I record: the operator's physical resilience offset a hostile environment.
Diplomat: The machine opened up to him... after he took far more than he should have to get there.
The system works again, despite the pain he went through without ever stopping.
The circuit accepted his adjustments. He never let go, even when it hurt.
The repair held. His physical determination eventually got the better of the machine itself.
Sentinel: Repair validated. Note: operator sustained a risk exposure without interrupting the task.
System stabilized despite a physical incident during the intervention. Endurance deemed off the charts.
Circuit operational after prolonged resistance to degraded working conditions.
Module functional. Report: resilience directly responsible for the operation continuing.
Explorer: It shocked me twice and I just kept going like nothing happened. Fixed anyway.
The thing fought back hard, but I take a hit pretty well, apparently.
I took a jolt that should have sent me flying. Still standing, still working.
That hurt, a lot. But I kept going, and there, it works again.

Analyst: Diagnostic impossible. Even high endurance has a limit against repeated malfunctions.
Repair failed. The operator's physical resilience delayed the failure, without preventing it.
Circuit unstable after prolonged exposure to degraded working conditions.
Problem unresolved. I record: perseverance alone wasn't enough to get past this malfunction.
Diplomat: The machine wouldn't cooperate... but he held on well after most would have given up.
The system rejected his adjustments, despite all his effort and resilience.
The circuit didn't accept his help. He gave everything, and it still wasn't enough.
The repair failed, despite remarkable endurance on his part.
Sentinel: Repair failed. Logging: prolonged resistance that ultimately didn't change the outcome.
System unstable. Report: repeated malfunctions eventually exceeded the tolerance threshold.
Circuit non-operational after prolonged exposure to a hazardous working environment.
Module out of service. I recommend reinforced protective equipment for the next intervention.
Explorer: I took a bunch of shocks, but at some point the machine just won. Ouch.
I usually handle shocks fine. That one was really too much.
I kept going well after I should have stopped. Result: it eventually got the better of me.
It knocked me flat, literally. Good attempt anyway.
